WOODBRIDGE ROAD, HONEYSUCKLE DRIVE, APPLEWOOD DRIVE, MEADOWLARK DRIVE AND LYNWOOD DRIVE ALL IN DOVER CONDOS COMPLEX HAD 15 LARGE TREES BLOWN DOWN. THREE UNITS HAD TREES THROUGH THE ROOF WITH ONE UNIT HAVING A TREE THROUGH THE ROOF OF A BEDROOM JUST MISSINTG A PERSON BY 2 FEET ON WOODBRIDGE ROAD. SEVERAL UNITS HAD SHINGLES BLOWN OFF THE ROOF. A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN SOMERS BETWEEN 611 AND 618 PM EDT. IT WAS RATED EF0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 75-80 MPH, A LENGTH OF 1.9 MILES, AND WIDTH OF APPROXIMATELY 75 YARDS. DOZENS OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. EYEWITNESSES SAW THEM FLYING BY. VIDEOS CONFIRMED THIS. TWO CARS WERE DESTROYED BY FALLING TREES. THERE WAS MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. DAMAGE BEGAN ON COLONIAL DR., MOVED ACROSS LAKE DR. AND MAIN ST., THEN ENDED ON HICKORY HILL DR.

TOP THIRD OF LARGE PINE TREE SNAPPED OFF AT THE 30 BLOCK OF RHODE ISLAND AVE. TOP OF TREE CAUSED DAMAGE TO A WORK VAN, BOAT, AND FENCE. SMALLER LIMBS LANDED ON THE POWER LINE AND CAUGHT FIRE. NATIONAL GRID DID CUT POWER FOR AN HOUR TO THE AREA TO REMOVE LIMBS FROM POWER LINE. NWS EMPLOYEE SPOKE WITH THE PROPERTY OWNER.

NWS CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MPH, WITH A WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. IT BEGAN NEAR QUINEBAUG ROAD AND RIVERSIDE DRIVE IN THOMPSON, CT. IT THEN TRAVELED NORTHEAST ON A DISCONTINUOUS PATH FOR 4.7 MILES, ENDING AS A WATERSPOUT ON LAKE CHAUBUNAGUNGAMAUG IN WEBSTER, MA. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED OR SNAPPED.

THE EF0 TORNADO THAT TOUCHED DOWN IN THOMPSON, CT AT 1015 AM CONTINUED NORTHEAST ON A DISCONTINUOUS PATH THROUGH WEBSTER, MA. AT 1021 AM, A TRAINED SPOTTER VIDEO TAPED THE TORNADO OVER THE WATER ON LAKE CHAUBUNAGUNGAMAUG, WHICH IS WHERE IT FINALLY DISSIPATED.
WATER SPOUT ON WEBSTER LAKE

A NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EFO TORNADO IN CLINTON, MA. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 35 YARDS AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.5 MILES. IT BEGAN NEAR CEDAR AND CHURCH STREETS, TRAVELED NORTHEAST, AND ENDED NEAR WATER STREET AT BEECH STREET. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED/SNAPPED. TREE DOWN ON HOUSE ON CALIFORNIA COURT AND SHINGLES OFF A ROOF ON BIRCH STREET, PER AMATEUR RADIO. TREES/WIRES ALSO DOWN ON NASHUA, GROVE, BENEFIT, PRESCOTT, WILLIAMS STREETS. NWS NORTON SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO BETWEEN 175 AND 198 MAIN ST IN BOLTON. THIS CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE CONSISTING OF TREES KNOCKED DOWN INTO POWERLINE WIRES AND A LARGE TREE BRANCH THAT WAS RIPPED FROM ANOTHER TREE. THE TORNADO TRAVELED A TENTH OF A MILE, WITH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. ESTIMATED WINDS WERE UP TO 65 MPH.

NWS NORTON SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO IN STOW AT 110 PM. THIS TORNADO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE ON STATE HIGHWAY 117 NEAR THE STOW POLICE DEPARTMENT. THE TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN A LARGE TREE AND A LARGE BRANCH. THIS TORNADO'S PATH LENGTH WAS A TENTH OF A MILE, WITH PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE UP TO 70 MPH.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS NEAR 75 MPH. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED IN A SMALL AREA NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF EAST BAY VIEW RD AND WAMPANOAG TRAIL. ONE HOME SUSTAINED DAMAGE WHERE A WINDOW OF A SUN PORCH WAS BLOWN IN AND OTHERS WERE BLOWN OUT. OUTSIDE PATIO FURNITURE WAS LOFTED ACROSS THE STREET. ANOTHER HOUSE ACROSS THE STREET HAD SHINGLES BLOWN OFF THE ROOF IN THE OPPOSITE DIRECTION FROM OTHER DAMAGE THAT WAS OBSERVED. THREE LARGE OAK TREES IN THE AREA WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN.
REPORT OF TREES AND WIRES DOWN ON FLANDERS ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

EF-0 WITH MAX WINDS 75MPH. TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SOUTH AND PUCKET STREETS, TRAVELED ENE AND CROSSED FLANDERS RD JUST NORTH OF NATHAN HALE DR. ON GROUND FOR ABOUT 90 SECONDS. PATH WAS NOT CONTINUOUS. DAMAGE WAS IN WOODED AREAS ON EITHER SIDE OF FLANDERS RD. LOTS OF TREES DAMAGED, SOME WITH SHEARED TOPS. DOZENS OF TREES BLOWN DOWN. NO DAMAGE TO NEARBY HOMES.
